candidates.quote: Ser Criston Cole abandoned Harrenhal, striking south along the western shore of the Gods Eye, with thirty-six-hundred men behind him
candidates.note: greens commander leading the host south from Harrenhal toward the ridge fight

candidates.quote: up on the ridge Red Robb Rivers and two of his archers raised their longbows. Three arrows flew across the field, striking Cole in belly, neck, and breast.
candidates.note: fires the killing shots on Criston Cole at Longleaf's instigation

candidates.quote: Three arrows flew across the field, striking Cole in belly, neck, and breast.
candidates.note: killed by Red Robb Rivers's arrows before the pitched battle even began; harm-gate ok (event.battle)

candidates.quote: But Longleaf the Lionslayer said, “I'll want three more,” and up on the ridge Red Robb Rivers and two of his archers raised their longbows.
candidates.note: blacks side, orders the archers to kill Criston rather than duel him personally — instigator, did not personally execute

candidates.quote: Lord Roderick raised a warhorn to his lips and sounded the charge, and the queen's men came screaming down the ridge
candidates.note: blacks side, sounds the charge that routs the greens

candidates.quote: Chief amongst them was Ser Garibald Grey in his dented plate and mail.
candidates.note: one of the three blacks Criston challenged to single/group combat before Longleaf called for archers instead; present combatant, later credited with coining “Butcher's Ball”

candidates.quote: while the Greatjon had seized the gold mines at Castamere, Nunn’s Deep, and the Pendric Hills.
candidates.note: the Greatjon personally executes the mine seizures during Robb's post-Oxcross westerlands campaign

candidates.quote: Since the battle, he sees no one, but broods in his Stone Drum.
candidates.note: the retreat itself happens off-page between ACOK and ASOS; this on-page dialogue (Salladhor Saan to Davos) confirms Stannis withdrew to Dragonstone and is now isolated there. NOT VICTIM_IN per rule (event.incident is not a harm subtype)

candidates.quote: only those knights that we took off at the end, and no ships but my little brave few
candidates.note: his Lysene ships evacuated Stannis's surviving men from the Blackwater — logistical/non-combat support role in the retreat

candidates.quote: They come round sniffing after you, my lord, but we saw them off, and buried three down by the turnips.
candidates.note: Sow's Horn garrison commander whose men repel and kill the Karstark raiders

candidates.quote: Lord Karstark has sworn to give the hand of his maiden daughter to any man highborn or low who brings him the head of the Kingslayer.
candidates.note: instigator — his bounty is what sends Karstark men raiding the riverlands/crownlands (including to Sow's Horn); did not personally execute the raid

candidates.quote: Every castle in Dorne, save Sunspear, was burned thrice over, as Balerion and Vhagar returned time and time again.
candidates.note: personally flies Balerion in the punitive two-year burning campaign

candidates.quote: Every castle in Dorne, save Sunspear, was burned thrice over, as Balerion and Vhagar returned time and time again.
candidates.note: personally flies Vhagar in the same burning campaign

candidates.quote: Queen Visenya was set upon one night in King’s Landing.
candidates.note: target of a Dornish assassination attempt during the Wroth period (she survived and cut the last attacker down herself — distinct AGENT_IN act not separately minted to avoid a redundant same-type role); harm-gate ok (event.battle)

candidates.quote: It was to them that Prince Jacaerys turned, at the urging of his fool, vowing that any man who could master a dragon would be granted lands and riches and dubbed a knight.
candidates.note: instigator/orderer of the Sowing — issues the call, does not personally attempt to mount a dragon

candidates.quote: Vermithor, the Old King’s own dragon, bent his neck to a blacksmith’s bastard, a towering man called Hugh the Hammer or Hard Hugh
candidates.note: personally mounts/tames Vermithor during the Sowing

candidates.quote: whilst a palehaired man-atarms named Ulf the White (for his hair) or Ulf the Sot (for his drinking) mounted Silverwing, beloved of Good Queen Alysanne.
candidates.note: personally mounts/tames Silverwing during the Sowing; “man-atarms” is a verbatim OCR artifact, not a typo

candidates.quote: And when Addam of Hull mounted Ser Laenor's dragon, Seasmoke, it seemed to prove the truth of his mother's claims.
candidates.note: personally mounts/tames Seasmoke during the Sowing

wiki_only.quote: Following his victory Robb divides his force to launch further attacks upon the westerlands.
wiki_only.note: overall campaign commander who divides his host to send the Greatjon (and others) raiding the westerlands after Oxcross; could not locate an on-page book quote tying Robb by name to specifically ordering the gold-mine seizures

flags: the-dragons-wroth: Wyl of Wyl's Cafferen-Oakheart wedding massacre (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04.md:131-132, 'Widow-lover... arrived uninvited at the wedding... slew Lord Oakheart and most of the wedding guests') is already SUB_BEAT_OF a dedicated node (wedding-raid-on-the-cafferen-oakheart-wedding per existing_edges) — his AGENT_IN role and any VICTIM_IN roles for Lord Oakheart/Alys Oakheart belong there, not minted on this parent summary node to avoid conflating events. Out of scope this packet.

the-sowing: Nettles's taming of Sheepstealer is already SUB_BEAT_OF a dedicated node (taming-of-sheepstealer-by-nettles per existing_edges) — her AGENT_IN role belongs there, not minted here to avoid conflating events. Out of scope this packet.

seizure-of-westerlands-gold-mines: existing_edges carry two PRECEDES links (in:PRECEDES:wedding-of-drogo-and-daenerys-targaryen, out:PRECEDES:purple-wedding) that look chronologically off — a 299 AC westerlands mine-seizure campaign 'preceding' Dany's AGOT wedding (~298 AC) doesn't track. Flagging for chronology review; not a role-edge issue so not touched here.

seizure-of-westerlands-gold-mines: Stafford Lannister's death at Oxcross belongs to the separate battle-of-oxcross node (which exists), not to this node — deliberately did not attach a VICTIM_IN here to avoid conflating the two distinct events.

butchers-ball: Aemond Targaryen was central to the surrounding campaign but split off from Criston's column before the battle itself ('Prince Aemond had already departed, flying Vhagar' — fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p03.md:103) to burn the riverlands solo instead of joining the fight at the ridge. No role minted for him on butchers-ball since the text places him elsewhere when it happened.

stannis-retreats-to-dragonstone and the-sowing: both event.incident — confirmed NO VICTIM_IN minted on either, per S213 override, even though the-sowing's underlying content (sixteen dead, burned/maimed seekers) reads harm-adjacent; the node subtype itself gates it.
